CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-13-2017 Continued To Dec-13-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Intervene With Respect To Its Interests As To The Claims Against Its Insured, Defendant Quy Dinh Pham
Off Calendar Oct-18-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Nathaniel Leeds, Esq. (415-839-8370) of Brent Fiol & Pratt LLP for Plaintiff.
William Hapiuk, Esq. (415-365-9804) of Clyde & Co. for Moving Party/Intervenor ACCEPTANCE INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY.
Jeffrey Allen, Esq. (415-352-6441) of Freeman Mathis Gary LLP for Intervenor ACCEPTANCE INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court announces the basis for the tentative ruling. Plaintiff argues and cites several cases. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

INTERVENOR ACCEPTANCE INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Intervene With Respect To Its Interests As To The Claims Against Its Insured, Defendant Quy Dinh Pham.

Proposed Intervenor Acceptance Indemnity Insurance Company's motion to intervene with respect to its interests as to the claims against its insured Quy Pham is granted. This motion presents an issue of first impression of whether prior to entry of judgment an insurer who previously refused to defend its insured but later provides a defense to the insured can intervene in a case to protect its own interests when the insured refuses to defend the case based on an agreement that was entered into between the plaintiffs and the insured at a time when the insurer refused to provide a defense to the insured. Based on principles from analogous cases, the court determines that, under the circumstances presented here -- where the insurer's decision to provide a defense without knowing of its insured's agreement with the plaintiffs, no trial date has been set, and the plaintiffs have not identified any legal prejudice to them by the insurer's intervention -- the insurer should be allowed to intervene . Allowing the insurer to intervene under these circumstances comports with CCP 387 and does not immunize the insurer from the consequences of its prior refusal to defend its insured if that prior refusal was wrongful because the insurer still faces liability for insurance bad faith, but not in this lawsuit. Nor does allowing the insurer to intervene vitiate the agreement entered into between the plaintiffs and the insured, although it arguably it now has diminished value to the plaintiffs because of the insurer's ability to contest the insured's liability and damages. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order signed in open court. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR No: 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-15-2016 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-16-2016 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Cross-defendants Linda Correnty and Kathrine Javier's demurrer to all three causes of action in the cross-complaint of Dmitry Erenkov is sustained without leave to amend as to all three causes of action. Ms. Correnty and Ms. Javier are correct that declaratory relief claims regarding indemnity and contribution do not lie where, as here, they are based on the alleged responsibility of plaintiffs' assignor for plaintiffs' damages. In such circumstances, the appropriate course is to allege one or more affirmative defenses of setoff based on the alleged direct or imputed wrongful conduct of the plaintiffs' assignor, not affirmative claims for relief. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim and show compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Kelley Anne DeYoung, CSR #6780. (302/HK)
